prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ electrophilic attack, f⁻ nucleophilic attack, and dual descriptor) for phenol; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ = 2.1028319 (atom 3, C).
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density-fitting (PySCF backend).
• Geometry provenance: fixed geometry read from user-supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz (no additional optimisation; single-point calculations on neutral (N), cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for N; doublet (1, 2) for the cation; doublet (–1, 2) for the anion.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF 2.x under the hood).
• Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for N, N±1 all reported converged; integrity monitor status ok, trustworthy true. All warnings echoed verbatim below.

Key numerical results
– Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C), f⁺ = 2.1028319  
– Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O), f⁻ = 0.16201477  

Reproducibility
Command invoked (see JSON ‘cli_invocation’):  
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpcrean2tf/live.json  
The engine’s live stdout/stderr log was streamed to the standard <skill>_<timestamp>.out file in the caller’s directory while the run executed; consult that file together with the result JSON for full SCF tables and optimizer details.

2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
